heading: Jurisdiction
label: 8852 Jurisdiction
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7652(a)(less applicability to capture).34 U.S.C. 1159(less applicability to capture), ; , ; , .Aug. 18, 1942, ch. 553, § 156 Stat. 746July 1, 1944, ch. 370, § 158 Stat. 678Nov. 14, 1945, ch. 472, § 159 Stat. 581(2d sentence).34 U.S.C. 1164(less 1st sentence), .Aug. 18, 1942, ch. 553, § 656 Stat. 7477652(c).34 U.S.C. 1166, .Aug. 18, 1942, ch. 553, § 856 Stat. 747.34 U.S.C. 1161, .Aug. 18, 1942, ch. 553, § 356 Stat. 746Subsection (a) reflects by restating the basic prize jurisdiction of that section over prizes brought into the United States, and by providing that the extension of prize jurisdiction conferred by on the United States district courts is exclusive of the courts of the States. and the second sentence of are executed in the single jurisdictional statement of this section and the consolidation of the , (6) with the earlier prize provisions. The words “during war” in are omitted as covered in . In clause (1) the words “or the Territories, Commonwealths, or possessions” are added, since “United States” in this title is geographically limited to the 48 States and the District of Columbia, whereas the term here is intended to include all places within the jurisdiction of the district courts.28 U.S.C. 133334 U.S.C. 115934 U.S.C. 116634 U.S.C. 1164Act of August 18, 1942, ch. 55356 Stat. 74634 U.S.C. 1159–11634 U.S.C. 1159§ 7651 of this titleIn clause (4) the words “taken or” preceding the words “appropriated for the use of the United States” are omitted as surplusage and in order to avoid confusion between the two meanings of the word “taken” in prize law. In both the Revised Statutes and the 1942 Act the phrase “taken or appropriated” means no more than “appropriated” alone, whereas “taken”, in the phrase “taken as prize” means “captured”.Subsection (b) is included to make the statement of jurisdiction complete. It is derived by implication from the first sentence of R.S. 4625 () which is the source of subsection (c) of .34 U.S.C. 1141§ 7653 of this title

Prior ProvisionsA prior section 8852, added , , ; amended , , ; , , ; , , , related to transfer or discharge of reserve major generals and brigadier generals with 35 years of service or five years in grade, prior to repeal by , , , effective .Pub. children.text: brought into the United States, or the Commonwealths or possessions;
brought into the territorial waters of a cobelligerent;
brought into a locality in the temporary or permanent possession of, or occupied by, the armed forces of the United States; or
appropriated for the use of the United States.
is lost or entirely destroyed; or
cannot be brought in for adjudication because of its condition.

children.label: c
children.text: The jurisdiction conferred by this section of prizes brought into the territorial waters of a cobelligerent may not be exercised, nor may prizes be appropriated for the use of the United States within those territorial waters, unless the government having jurisdiction over those waters consents to the exercise of the jurisdiction or to the appropriation.
